title: Christoph_hassa_02_Flow_Field_Mixing_Characteristics_of_an_Aero-Engine_Combustor
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 3]
Here's the technical content extracted from the provided text:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**
*   g(x,y) ~ Δt * Psca(x,y) * Np(x,y) (1)
*   C1(x,y) = (m1 / (m1 + m2))x,y (2)
*   C1(x,y) = (M1 / (M1 + M2))x,y (3)
*   Np(x,y) = Cp1 * M1(x,y) (4)
*   (M1 + M2)x,y = ρ1(x,y) * Δx * Δy * Δz (5)
*   g(x,y) ~ Δt * Psca(x,y) * Cp1 * ρ1(x,y) * Δx * Δy * Δz(y) * C1(x,y) (6)
*   Psca(x,y) ~ Csca(dp,x) * (Pl(x,y) / (Δy * Δz(y))) (7)
*   g(x,y) ~ Δt * Csca(dp,x) * Pl(x,y) * Cp1 * ρ(x,y) * Δx * C1(x,y) (8)

**2. Graphs/Charts:**
NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**3. Tables:**
NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**
NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Δt: Exposure time
*   Δx, Δy: Area element in the light sheet
*   Δz: Light sheet thickness
*   Np(x,y): Number of particles
*   Cp: Particle concentration
*   ρ(x,y): Local fluid density
*   g(x,y): Grey level
*   Psca(x,y): Scattering power
*   C1: Mass concentration of component one
*   m1, m2: Mass of flow components
*   M1, M2: Total mass portions within the volume element
*   Cp1: Particle concentration of flow component 1
*   Csca(dp,x): Scattering cross-section as a function of particle diameter and scattering direction
*   Pl(x,y): Local laser power
*   (x0, y0): Reference point

title: Christoph_hassa_02_Flow_Field_Mixing_Characteristics_of_an_Aero-Engine_Combustor
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 4]
Here's a breakdown of the technical content extracted from the provided text:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   g(x,y) / g(x0,y0) = (Csca(dp,x) * P(x,y)) / (Csca(dp,x0) * P(x0,y0)) * (p1(x,y) * C1(x,y)) / (p1(x0,y0) * C1(x0,y0))
*   Csca(dp,x) / Csca(dp,x0) = Csca(x) / Csca(x0)
*   g(x,y) / g(x0,y0) = (Csca(x) * P(x,y)) / (Csca(x0) * P(x0,y0)) * (p1(x,y) * C1(x,y)) / p1(x0,y0)
*   C1(x,y) = Csca(x0) / Csca(x) * (P(x0,y0) / P(x,y)) * (g(x,y) / g(x0,y0))
*   PL(x,y) = PL(y)

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   Figure 4 refers to a plot of scattering intensity I(theta) as a function of the scattering angle theta.  Axes are:
    *   X-axis: Scattering angle (theta)
    *   Y-axis: Scattering Intensity (I)
    *   Units: No units specified.

**3. Tables:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   Figure 4 shows a typical QLS (Quantitative Laser Sheet) setup with:
    *   CCD camera,
    *   light sheet,
    *   region G (of light sheet),
    *   scattered light particles.
    *   positions x

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   The concentration C1(x0,y0) in the core of the seeded jet flow is set to 1.0, corresponding to 100%.
*   dp - particle diameter
*   Csca - scattering cross section
*   PL - Laser power distribution
*   p1 - density field
*   C1 - concentration
*   I - scattering intensity
*   theta - scattering angle

Please note that the notation and meaning of the variables are inferred from the surrounding text, as a legend or definitions are not explicitly provided.

**1. Mathematical Equations:** NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**2. Graphs/Charts:** NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**3. Tables:**

*   **Table 1: Main error sources and their influence on mixing fraction mean value**

    | Error source           | Error (in percent) Before Correction | Error (in percent) After Correction | Method of Correction                                                   |
    | :---------------------- | :------------------------------------ | :----------------------------------- | :--------------------------------------------------------------------- |
    | Background / reflections | 20%                                   | < 1%                                  | Subtraction of background image suppression of reflections by painting |
    | Pixel sensitivity      | 9%                                    | <1%                                   | Division by sensitivity image                                         |
    | Light sheet intensity distribution| 25%                              | <2%                                   | Division by reference profile                                          |
    | Scattering directions   | Up to 50% depending on image size           | <2%                                   | Division by calibration function                                       |
    | Extinction             | <10%                                   | <2%                                   | Correction by shooting method                                          |
    | Multiple scattering    | Max 5%                               | No correction 5% | Not available                                                       |

**4. Diagrams:** NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Error (in percent)
    *   Before Correction: 9%, 20%, 25%, Up to 50%, <10%, Max 5%
    *   After Correction: <1%, <2%
